description: Adversarial invariant for the manual remote-patient-monitoring and continuous-glucose-monitor audit: sensor devices should depend on biosensors, while RPM should depend on transmitted physiological monitoring rather than molecular diagnostics.

checks.reason: CGMs are sensor devices that can feed RPM; they should not depend on the broader remote monitoring workflow.
CGMs require electronic sensing, processing, and readout.
CGMs should stay anchored to the first FDA-approved CGM chronology.
Glucose biosensors should stay anchored to the Clark/Lyons enzyme-electrode concept.
RPM centers on transmitted patient-monitoring data, not molecular diagnostic assays.
Ambulatory physiological monitoring is the patient-data lineage for RPM.
Connected-device infrastructure scales RPM deployment rather than serving as the physiological sensor itself.
Ambulatory physiological monitoring should stay anchored to early clinical Holter monitoring.
